# V4 — Known issues

Bugs that are tracked but not yet fixed. Each entry lists a clear cause
and the path to fix so the next person can pick it up without
re-diagnosing.

---

## Player banner / signature renders tofu (boxes-with-x) for Cyrillic names

**Status:** acknowledged, not yet fixed. User opted to leave broken
during the dark-mode pass.

**Symptom:** Player signature image generated by the backend banner
service shows boxes-with-a-cross instead of the decoded player name
when the name decodes to Cyrillic glyphs.

**Cause:** the embedded TTFs at
`api/PlayerBanners/Resources/Roboto-Bold.ttf` and `Roboto-Medium.ttf`
are Latin-only subsets (33 KB each). Full Roboto with Cyrillic +
Latin Extended is ~170–200 KB. `PlayerNameDecoder.Decode(...)` is
already wired correctly in `BannerRenderer.cs:74` — the issue is that
the font doesn't have glyphs for the decoded characters, so the
SixLabors text renderer paints the missing-glyph `.notdef` box.

**Fix paths:**

1. **Replace the TTFs.** Drop a full multilingual Roboto-Bold +
   Roboto-Medium into `api/PlayerBanners/Resources/`, keeping the
   filenames. `BannerFonts.cs` will pick them up unchanged. Roboto is
   Apache 2.0 — Google Fonts is the canonical source.
2. **Add a Cyrillic fallback font.** Bundle Noto Sans (Apache 2.0,
   broad Unicode coverage) alongside Roboto. Update `BannerFonts.cs` to
   load both and pass the fallback into SixLabors' `TextOptions.FallbackFonts`
   list — the renderer will use Noto when Roboto has no glyph.

Either approach is contained to the `api/PlayerBanners/` folder.

---

## Adding new known issues

Add a new `##` section per issue with the same structure:
- Status / Symptom / Cause / Fix paths.
- Keep them concise — the diagnosis goes here, the actual fix goes in
  the codebase.
